blurb: Christ has set us free not for the burden of new rules but for the joy of authentic relationship with the Father. To return to the law after tasting such grace is to trade sonship for slavery, to exchange the Father's embrace for the taskmaster's demands. Paul's stark warning echoes with pastoral urgency: we cannot have both the freedom of faith and the security of legal performance—one inevitably cancels the other. The yoke of Christ is easy precisely because it rests not on our shoulders but on his.

verses.text: Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.
Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.
I testify that a man that is circumcised is a debtor to do the whole Law,
Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.
